title: Repository Purpose, Risk Profile, and Definition of Done · Gate Defense
summary: Design quality gates for a new repository according to its actual failure costs. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a repository engineering contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime.
Rank correctness, security, accessibility, performance, compatibility, and maintenance risks.
Define machine-verifiable and human-reviewed completion criteria.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Repo Purpose · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Use a changed quiz case for gates risk contract that tests this competency: Define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Which action demonstrates Repo Purpose without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Exception Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Use a changed quiz case for gates risk contract that tests this competency: Document bounded suppressions, owners, expiry, and review evidence.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
steps.why: Retrieving Exception Policy through the concrete a repository engineering contract case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Document bounded suppressions, owners, expiry, and review evidence.
